# `minimal` Function

Remove null, empty string, and empty collection values from a list or map

Returns the input list or map with all null values, empty strings, and empty collections (lists, maps, objects) removed.

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
minimal(input dynamic) dynamic
```

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `input` (Dynamic) The list or map to minimize.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->


## Example Usage

```console
> provider::util::minimal(["a", null, "", []])
tolist(["a"])

> provider::util::minimal({ a = "keep", b = null, c = [], d = {}, e = "" })
tomap({ a = "keep" })

> provider::util::minimal({ x = [1, 2], y = [], z = null })
tomap({ x = tolist([1, 2]) })
```

~> **Note:** `minimal` removes null values, empty strings, and empty collections (`[]`, `{}`), but keeps `false` and `0`. Use `compact` to only strip nulls and empty strings while keeping empty collections.

### HCL Example

```terraform
locals {
  config = {
    api     = { port = 8080 }
    web     = null
    metrics = {}
    cache   = []
  }
}

# Returns {api = {port = 8080}} — nulls and empty collections removed
output "minimal_config" {
  value = provider::util::minimal(local.config)
}
# Result:
# {
#   api = { port = 8080 }
# }
```
